title: BBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management
content: The BBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialization addresses the critical need for proficient professionals who can optimize the flow of goods and services from source to consumption. This program offers a comprehensive study of supply chain networks, including procurement, inventory management, warehousing, transportation, and distribution. Students will learn to leverage technology and analytical tools to enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and build resilient supply chains in an increasingly interconnected global economy. With India's focus on infrastructure development and e-commerce growth, the demand for skilled logistics and supply chain managers is immense. Graduates will be equipped to manage complex logistics operations across various industries, contributing significantly to organizational success and customer satisfaction through strategic planning and operational excellence. Course Category | Credits | Common Core | 54 | Specialisation Core | 18 | Specialisation Elective | 18 | VAD | 8 | AEC | 12 | Community Service Project | 2 | Internship | 5 | Industrial Research Project | 3 | Total | 120 | XII with minimum 50% marks in Aggregate. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) BBA (Logistics and Supply Chain Management) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 01 Excel as efficient operational coordinators in logistics, warehousing, and supply chain functions, applying core business principles to optimize the flow of goods and services across diverse industries. 02 Promote sustainable and ethical supply chain practices, contributing to resilient and responsible value chains for businesses and society. 03 Adapt to technological advancements and global trends in supply chain management, pursuing continuous learning to progress into analytical or supervisory roles within logistics operations. 01 Evaluate supply chain processes from procurement to distribution, applying principles of inventory management, warehousing, and transportation to optimize efficiency and reduce costs. 02 Utilize digital tools and analytical techniques specific to logistics and supply chain management to design resilient and sustainable supply chain solutions. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 XII with minimum 50% marks in Aggregate. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) BBA (Logistics and Supply Chain Management) Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level
